signal.qualitative_note: TAM ceiling risk — data centre GPU TAM ~$300B est.; NVDA at ~$190B annualised DC revenue = ~63% penetration; meaningful ceiling risk at current trajectory.

model.growth_cap_note: rev_cagr_3y of 100% is FY23-base-effect inflation (pre-AI revenue base). Spec fallback ceiling for pre-profit is 35%; today's run preserves prior methodology (uncapped trailing CAGR) for IV continuity per slice-3 acceptance criterion. NVDA needs playbooks/NVDA.md with growth_ceiling: 0.35 (and confidence_anchor: MEDIUM) to be spec-compliant going forward (follow-up).

bear.narrative: revenue miss, custom-silicon (ASIC) penetration accelerates, exit multiple compresses to AVGO/AMD weighted average, terminal margin compresses 500bps as competition intensifies

base.narrative: consensus revenue trajectory holds, AI-data-centre TAM expands faster than hyperscaler capex deceleration, exit multiple at comp-set median, terminal margin sustains at current run rate

bull.narrative: TAM expands to include physical/edge AI, AVGO/ASML-tier exit multiple, terminal margin expands as software/networking attach lifts ASPs, sovereign AI demand provides volume floor

sensitivity.note: Exit multiple ±1.0× absolute moves base IV by ~±9%; dilution ±200bps moves IV by ~±10% (close second); WACC ±100bps ±8%; terminal margin ±300bps <±1% (exit EV is revenue × multiple, not FCF × multiple, so margin only affects the small explicit-FCF PV). Caveat: revenue base-year effect (FY23 pre-AI) inflates base & bull; bear IV ($381) is the more defensible reference point.

position_sizing.rationale: BUY × MARGIN OF SAFETY (price $222.32 vs base IV $2263.92, MoS +918%) → 4–6% band, capped to 4–4% by MEDIUM confidence (pre-profit variant always caps at MEDIUM). Caveat: base IV inflated by trailing 3y revenue CAGR (100%, FY23 pre-AI base-effect) — size off the bear IV ($381.26, MoS +72%), not base.
